question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Cache{}] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML config with Cache.MaxSize set to 0, causing the entity cache to grow without bound under normal API load, exhausting process memory and crashing the Rosetta service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Cache{}] Can an unprivileged external user set Cache.MaxSize to a negative integer in the YAML config, triggering a panic or undefined behavior in the underlying cache library when the Rosetta service initializes, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Pool{}] Can an unprivileged external user set Pool.MaxOpenConnections to 0 in the config (which Go's database/sql treats as unlimited), causing the Rosetta service to open unbounded DB connections under load, exhausting the database server and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Pool{}] Can an unprivileged external user set Pool.MaxIdleConnections to a value greater than Pool.MaxOpenConnections, causing Go's sql pool to behave unexpectedly and leak idle connections, exhausting file descriptors and causing a non-network DoS?
